NKJV.1: A Psalm of David when he fled from Absalom his son. LORD, how they have increased who trouble me! Many are they who rise up against me.
NKJV.2: Many are they who say of me, “There is no help for him in God.” Selah
NKJV.3: But You, O LORD, are a shield for me, My glory and the One who lifts up my head.
NKJV.4: I cried to the LORD with my voice, And He heard me from His holy hill. Selah
NKJV.5: I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the LORD sustained me.
NKJV.6: I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people Who have set themselves against me all around.
NKJV.7: Arise, O LORD; Save me, O my God!  For You have struck all my enemies on the cheekbone; You have broken the teeth of the ungodly.
NKJV.8: Salvation belongs to the LORD. Your blessing is upon Your people. Selah

ESV.1: O LORD, how many are my foes! Many are rising against me;
ESV.2: many are saying of my soul, “There is no salvation for him in God.” Selah
ESV.3: But you, O LORD, are a shield about me, my glory, and the lifter of my head.
ESV.4: I cried aloud to the LORD, and he answered me from his holy hill. Selah
ESV.5: I lay down and slept; I woke again, for the LORD sustained me.
ESV.6: I will not be afraid of many thousands of people who have set themselves against me all around.
ESV.7: Arise, O LORD! Save me, O my God! For you strike all my enemies on the cheek; you break the teeth of the wicked.
ESV.8: Salvation belongs to the LORD; your blessing be on your people! Selah

NASB.1: A Psalm of David, when he fled from Absalom his son. O LORD, how my adversaries have increased! Many are rising up against me.
NASB.2: Many are saying of my soul, 'There is no deliverance for him in God.' Selah.
NASB.3: But You, O LORD, are a shield about me, My glory, and the One who lifts my head.
NASB.4: I was crying to the LORD with my voice, And He answered me from His holy mountain. Selah.
NASB.5: I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the LORD sustains me.
NASB.6: I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people Who have set themselves against me round about.
NASB.7: Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God! For You have smitten all my enemies on the cheek; You have shattered the teeth of the wicked.
NASB.8: Salvation belongs to the LORD; Your blessing [be] upon Your people! Selah.

NLT.1:
O LORD, I have so many enemies;
so many are against me.
NLT.2:
So many are saying,
“God will never rescue him!” Interlude
NLT.3:
But you, O LORD, are a shield around me;
you are my glory, the one who holds my head high.
NLT.4:
I cried out to the LORD,
and he answered me from his holy mountain. Interlude
NLT.5:
I lay down and slept,
yet I woke up in safety,
for the LORD was watching over me.
NLT.6:
I am not afraid of ten thousand enemies
who surround me on every side.
NLT.7:
Arise, O LORD!
Rescue me, my God!
Slap all my enemies in the face!
Shatter the teeth of the wicked!
NLT.8:
Victory comes from you, O LORD.
May you bless your people. Interlude

WEB.1: A Psalm by David, when he fled from Absalom his son. Yahweh, how my adversaries have increased! Many are those who rise up against me.
WEB.2: Many there are who say of my soul, “There is no help for him in God.” Selah.
WEB.3: But you, Yahweh, are a shield around me, my glory, and the one who lifts up my head.
WEB.4: I cry to Yahweh with my voice, and he answers me out of his holy hill. Selah.
WEB.5: I laid myself down and slept. I awakened; for Yahweh sustains me.
WEB.6: I will not be afraid of tens of thousands of people who have set themselves against me on every side.
WEB.7: Arise, Yahweh! Save me, my God! For you have struck all of my enemies on the cheek bone. You have broken the teeth of the wicked.
WEB.8: Salvation belongs to Yahweh. Your blessing be on your people. Selah.

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. When he fled from Absalom his son. O LORD, how my enemies have increased!
Many are rising up against me.
AMP.2:
Many are saying of me,
"There is no help [no salvation] for him in God." Selah.
AMP.3:
But You, O LORD, are a shield for me,
My glory [and my honor], and the One who lifts my head.
AMP.4:
With my voice I was crying to the LORD,
And He answered me from His holy mountain. Selah.
AMP.5:
I lay down and slept [safely];
I awakened, for the LORD sustains me.
AMP.6:
I will not be intimidated or afraid of the ten thousands
Who have set themselves against me all around.
AMP.7:
Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God!
For You have struck all my enemies on the cheek;
You have shattered the teeth of the wicked.
AMP.8:
Salvation belongs to the LORD;
May Your blessing be upon Your people. Selah.

MSG.1:
A David psalm, when he escaped for his life from Absalom, his son.
God! Look! Enemies past counting!
Enemies sprouting like mushrooms,
MSG.2:
Mobs of them all around me, roaring their mockery:
"Hah! No help for him from God!"
MSG.3:
But you, God, shield me on all sides;
You ground my feet, you lift my head high;
MSG.4:
With all my might I shout up to God,
His answers thunder from the holy mountain.
MSG.5:
I stretch myself out. I sleep.
Then I'm up again—rested, tall and steady,
MSG.6:
Fearless before the enemy mobs
Coming at me from all sides.
MSG.7:
Up, God! My God, help me!
Slap their faces,
First this cheek, then the other,
Your fist hard in their teeth!
MSG.8:
Real help comes from God.
Your blessing clothes your people!

NIV.1:
Psalm 3
A psalm of David. When he fled from his son Absalom.
O Lord, how many are my foes!
How many rise up against me!
NIV.2:
Many are saying of me,
“God will not deliver him.” Selah
NIV.3:
But you are a shield around me, O Lord;
you bestow glory on me and lift up my head.
NIV.4:
To the Lord I cry aloud,
and he answers me from his holy hill. Selah
NIV.5:
I lie down and sleep;
I wake again, because the Lord sustains me.
NIV.6:
I will not fear the tens of thousands
drawn up against me on every side.
NIV.7:
Arise, O Lord!
Deliver me, O my God!
Strike all my enemies on the jaw;
break the teeth of the wicked.
NIV.8:
From the Lord comes deliverance.
May your blessing be on your people. Selah

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, how many are my foes!
Many are rising against me;
NRSVCE.2:
many are saying to me,
“There is no help for you in God.” Selah
NRSVCE.3:
But you, O LORD, are a shield around me,
my glory, and the one who lifts up my head.
NRSVCE.4:
I cry aloud to the LORD,
and he answers me from his holy hill. Selah
NRSVCE.5:
I lie down and sleep;
I wake again, for the LORD sustains me.
NRSVCE.6:
I am not afraid of ten thousands of people
who have set themselves against me all around.
NRSVCE.7:
Rise up, O LORD!
Deliver me, O my God!
For you strike all my enemies on the cheek;
you break the teeth of the wicked.
NRSVCE.8:
Deliverance belongs to the LORD;
may your blessing be on your people! Selah

NET.1: LORD, how numerous are my enemies! Many attack me.
NET.2: Many say about me, “God will not deliver him.” (Selah)
NET.3: But you, LORD, are a shield that protects me; you are my glory and the one who restores me.
NET.4: To the LORD I cried out, and he answered me from his holy hill. (Selah)
NET.5: I rested and slept; I awoke, for the LORD protects me.
NET.6: I am not afraid of the multitude of people who attack me from all directions.
NET.7: Rise up, LORD! Deliver me, my God! Yes, you will strike all my enemies on the jaw; you will break the teeth of the wicked.
NET.8: The LORD delivers; you show favor to your people. (Selah)
